meaning: 本格拉[安哥拉西部港市]
examples: The famed Benguela railway across Angola ground to a halt because of the civil war and foreign invasion, meaning much of it was destroyed.
Once in the Atlantic they tried in vain to head east again, all the while being taken up the west coast of Africa by the cold Benguela Current.
From the crest there is a view to the ocean, three miles distant, shrouded in fog created by the convergence of desert heat and the icy Benguela Current.
Then the Benguela current forced the diamond-bearing sands ashore, forming the Namib desert.
Tearing up from Antarctica, the trade winds of the Benguela system batter the shoreline night and day.
